title: Gymnadenia keiskei
intro: Gymnadenia keiskei, also known as Keiske's orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various conditions such as fever, colds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia keiskei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnadenia keiskei is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in the form of pills, granules, and other solid preparations.
taxonomy: Gymnadenia keiskei is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Gymnadenia and the subtribe Orchidinae. Gymnadenia keiskei is native to East Asia and has been classified into the family Orchidaceae based on its morphological characteristics. The species is further categorized into the subgenus Gymnadenia and the section Gymnadenia. The classification of Gymnadenia keiskei has been widely accepted among botanists and taxonomists.
